title: Beginer Guide Next.js
summary: A comprehensive guide for beginners on the Next.js JavaScript framework for building fast and scalable server-side rendering web applications.

body.raw:
## Introduction to Next.js

Next.js is a JavaScript framework for building fast and scalable server-side rendering web applications. It is built on top of React, one of the most popular front-end libraries for building user interfaces, and Node.js, a JavaScript runtime for server-side programming. Next.js makes it easier to build web applications with a modern architecture and helps developers to focus on writing high-quality code and building great user experiences.

## What is Server-side Rendering?

Server-side rendering (SSR) is a way of rendering web pages on the server instead of the client. This technique has become increasingly popular in recent years due to the many benefits it provides, such as improved performance, better SEO, and a better user experience.

With SSR, the web page is pre-rendered on the server and then sent to the client, where it is displayed in the browser. This means that the client does not have to wait for the JavaScript to load and execute before the page can be displayed, resulting in faster page load times and a better user experience.

## Why use Next.js?

There are many reasons why you should consider using Next.js for your next web application. Some of the key benefits include:

- **Performance:** Next.js is designed for performance and provides a fast and efficient way of delivering web pages to the client. With SSR, the pages are pre-rendered on the server, reducing the load time and providing a better user experience.

- **Easy to learn:** Next.js is built on top of React, so if you are familiar with React, you will find it easy to learn and use Next.js. Additionally, Next.js provides a great developer experience with features such as automatic code splitting, optimized asset loading, and hot code reloading.

- **Scalability:** Next.js is designed for scalability and can handle high traffic websites with ease. It provides a modern architecture that allows you to easily add new features and scale your application as needed.

- **SEO friendly:** Next.js provides server-side rendering, which makes it easier for search engines to index your website. This results in better search engine optimization (SEO) and helps to drive more traffic to your website.

- **Production ready:** Next.js is production-ready and provides a robust and stable platform for building web applications. It has a large and active community of developers who are constantly working on new features and improvements.

## Getting started with Next.js

Getting started with Next.js is easy, and you can be up and running in just a few minutes. To create a new Next.js application, you can use the following steps:

1. Install Node.js and npm (Node Package Manager).
2. Create a new directory for your project and navigate to it in the terminal.
3. Run the following command to create a new Next.js application:
